title: gh#52: Date picker icon barely visible on new-post page in dark mode
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/52.
Backlog section: Bugs.

## Summary

On the new-post page (Twitter compose), the native date-picker icon next to the **Schedule** field is nearly invisible against the dark background. The icon renders in a dark color that has poor contrast with the surrounding input/page background.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open the app in dark mode.
2. Click **New Post**, select Twitter as the channel.
3. Scroll to the **Schedule** field.
4. Observe the calendar/picker icon on the right edge of the input.

## Expected

The picker icon has enough contrast against the dark input background to be clearly visible (WCAG AA, 3:1 minimum for non-text UI components).

## Actual

The icon blends into the input background — visible only when you know where to look.

## Screenshot

(See attached image — `mm/dd/yyyy --:-- --` field with a near-invisible icon at the right edge.)

## Notes

- Likely the browser default `::-webkit-calendar-picker-indicator` color, which doesn't adapt to dark themes.
- Fix candidates: invert the indicator with `filter: invert(1)` in dark mode, ship a custom date-picker component, or override the indicator color via CSS to match the field's text/foreground token.
recommendation: Implement the fix described by GitHub issue gh#52 and satisfy its acceptance criteria.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Imported from GitHub issue. Preserve or add focused regression coverage when the issue acceptance criteria calls for it.

history.reasoning: The finding had no original evidence paths, but the current new-post page still renders SharedPostFields at packages/web/src/pages/posts/NewPostPage.tsx:572, and SharedPostFields still renders the Schedule control as a native Input with type="datetime-local" at packages/web/src/components/posts/SharedPostFields.tsx:115-125. The shared Input styling at packages/web/src/components/ui/input.tsx:15-16 sets a dark background and foreground text but does not set color-scheme or style the native calendar picker indicator. A repo-wide search found no ::-webkit-calendar-picker-indicator rule and no relevant color-scheme rule under packages/web. Existing tests cover schedule validation/focus and datetime conversion, not picker icon contrast. Attempting the targeted web test was blocked by the read-only sandbox because Vite tried to create packages/web/node_modules/.vite-temp. With the native datetime control still present and no CSS/custom-picker fix in current code, the issue remains open.

The original finding had no evidence paths to re-check, but the relevant current path still exists: NewPostPage renders SharedPostFields at packages/web/src/pages/posts/NewPostPage.tsx:572, and SharedPostFields still renders the Schedule control as a native datetime-local input at packages/web/src/components/posts/SharedPostFields.tsx:115-125. The current working tree now adds a dark-mode fix in packages/web/src/index.css:145-151: dark datetime-local inputs get color-scheme: dark, and the WebKit calendar picker indicator gets filter: invert(1) with opacity 0.85 under :root:not([data-theme="light"]). That directly addresses the reported near-invisible native picker icon in dark mode. A targeted Vitest run could not start because the read-only sandbox blocked Vite from writing packages/web/node_modules/.vite-temp, but a read-only static regression assertion passed, confirming both the native datetime-local schedule input and the dark picker-indicator CSS are present in the current repository. git status also shows packages/web/src/index.css modified, so this fix is present in the current working tree rather than the prior baseline.
